mnemonics: CHURCH-TURING THESIS: Every function that can be computed by an algorithm is computable by a Turing Machine.
PDA STACK: Push Down Automata use a stack to remember context (like matching parentheses).
DECIDABLE? Ask: 'Can a Turing Machine always halt with a yes/no answer for this problem?'
keyPoints: A language is decidable if a Turing Machine exists that accepts it and always halts.
The Church-Turing Thesis is a hypothesis about the nature of computability, not a proven theorem.
A Pushdown Automaton (PDA) is more powerful than a Finite Automaton due to its infinite stack memory.
commonMistakes: Assuming a language is decidable just because it is recursively enumerable (it might only be semi-decidable).
Confusing the capabilities of Finite Automata (no memory) with Pushdown Automata (stack memory).
Believing the Church-Turing Thesis is a formal, mathematical proof rather than a foundational postulate.
